proven.claim: PROVEN: Guard Narrowing at addPositive is prevented (CEGAR-refined encoding)
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: guard-narrowing
; LINE: 31
; REASON: The original encoding forced guard_condition=0 (guard never fires), making
; code_after_reached=1 trivially satisfiable — that is just normal execution, not a
; violation. The real violation to check is whether code_after_reached can be 1 when
; guard_condition=1 (guard fires/throws). Two constraints were missing: (a) assert
; guard_condition=1 to probe the violation scenario, and (b) encode that when the guard
; throws (guard_returns=1) code_after is unreachable (guard_returns=1 => code_after=0).
(declare-const guard_condition Int)
(declare-const guard_returns Int)
(declare-const code_after_reached Int)
; if guard fires, it throws
(assert (=> (= guard_condition 1) (= guard_returns 1)))
; if guard does NOT throw, code after is reached
(assert (=> (= guard_returns 0) (= code_after_reached 1)))
; ADDED: if guard throws, code after is NOT reached
(assert (=> (= guard_returns 1) (= code_after_reached 0)))
; probe the violation: guard fires (a<0 || b<0 is true)
(assert (= guard_condition 1))
; claim: code after guard is still reached — this should be UNSAT
(assert (= code_after_reached 1))
(check-sat)
proven.reason: The original encoding forced guard_condition=0 (guard never fires), making code_after_reached=1 trivially satisfiable — that is just normal execution, not a violation. The real violation to check is whether code_after_reached can be 1 when guard_condition=1 (guard fires/throws). Two constraints were missing: (a) assert guard_condition=1 to probe the violation scenario, and (b) encode that when the guard throws (guard_returns=1) code_after is unreachable (guard_returns=1 => code_after=0).
proven.judge_note: cegar-flipped: violation became proof after tightening encoding

clause_history.clause:
; PRINCIPLE: guard-narrowing
; LINE: 31
; VIOLATION: Guard Narrowing at addPositive
(declare-const guard_condition Int)
(declare-const guard_returns Int)
(declare-const code_after_reached Int)
(assert (=> (= guard_condition 1) (= guard_returns 1)))
(assert (=> (= guard_returns 0) (= code_after_reached 1)))
(assert (= guard_condition 0))
(assert (= code_after_reached 1))
(check-sat)
